Hello everyone and welcome to the simple client accountability system. So this system that I'm about to show you is so high-tech that NASA engineered it. They did not actually engineer it. I spent like $30,000 on software that was supposed to do what this does and this does it better. And all my trainers liked it better. So let me save you $30,000 and show you the world's simplest system that works. So there are multiple sheets that I'm going to show you and these are in sequence of how a customer would experience them. So the first sheet is a sign-up sheet. The reason it's called a sign-up sheet is that this is actually a sign-in sheet but I believe in like subconsciously planning as many kind of assumed closes that someone is going to do it. So when that person walks in the door, they're putting their name or phone number, whether they have appointment yes or no, which automatically makes people feel, I just think it makes them feel things. And then it says sign-up. Now ideally what you can also do here is have, I mean if you haven't a stack scheduled then this will just be full on it. So it looked like it was absolutely packed with sign-ups. So people would see that just from that day or I'd have two or three sheets. They'd have to flip through before they'd find the next empty space, which just shows scarcity, that there's a lot of people who are signing up for this doohickey. So this is the first thing that someone would see. This is before they actually become a client. So I like to have this clipboard like in the waiting room and be like, you know, as soon as someone walks in, hey, can you sign in? We'll be right with you, etc. Right? And then you'd hand them the before and after books that they can flip through that while they wait. After they purchase whatever program you're doing on the front end, I like having a weigh-in schedule. And so you'll notice here that I have one hour blocks, but I like to space it out in 30, like two per 30 minutes. I like having extra room on here. You can reformat this how you like. I mean, it's simple. It's an Excel sheet that we just printed out. But if you want to maximize your selling opportunities 30 minutes, which means that probably within three hours you can meet with 12 new customers. And if you do that Saturday Sunday, maybe 24, as an additional note, Monday night is when we did make-ups typically. So that way we could consolidate all of our time. So we'd have Monday nights and Saturday mornings would be our accountability system times. You can start earlier in the day than nine o'clock. Typically if you have a customer, they're going to come in. They'll be willing to wake up earlier in order to meet with you. Whereas if you have sales appointments on Saturdays, you'd want those to be at more ideal times. So you might even start this at 7 a.m. at 10. So that's up to you. As an additional note, make sure that you separate the no-sale sale clients from the customers who have put deposits down. So you might want to save one of these blocks, maybe 11 to 12 o'clock for the four people or the eight people or whatever who have signed up for the no-sale sale. An additional note with the no-sale sale, with those ones you can schedule four per 30 minute because half of them will not show. It'll be the same show rate. It'll be the same expected show rate of a normal appointment and you'll still need to remind them the day before, which you should do anyways even for sign-ups. So just a reminder there. And I also like the two per 30 minutes because it gives you a little bit more personalization with the experience. You get to meet everyone. They feel like they're heard. You build a relationship, which is the whole point of this and will increase the likelihood that they stay. So sometimes big groups can be a little bit more impersonal and you definitely will not sell as many supplements. So I recommend you do it this way. Okay. The next sheet is the accountability system sheet, which is the weekly weigh-in sheet. So now they are a customer. You can see week zero, which is their first weigh-in. Week one, week two, week three, week four, week five. Each week they will put in their weight or body fat depending on what they are tracking. And put a special star for all the people who are body fat percentage. So let me erase what I just said about the body fat percentage. Although weigh-ins, you'd want them to do weekly. Weigh-ins for the body fat percentage, I had them go the first week, week three, and then week six because the day-to-day adjustments on like an Omron body fat scanner can be hazardous. You know what I mean? Someone's water weight can fluctuate more than that will throw off the reading in a short between a short time window. So one week would be a short time window. So have them, if they are doing body fat do week zero, week three, and week five or six. And you can still have them track their weight this whole period of time as well and just put asterisks next to them so that you know that they are body fat percentage challengers. Here we have Voxer on this example. We used Voxer. You do not need to use that. You can just have their cell phone number there so that you can text them from the front app, a setup that we already did in the Legion side. So you can text them from there to check in with them from your computer so it's a little bit easier or you can do it from a burner phone. It is entirely up to you. But this way you can have their name, their cell right there. You can know what their program is and you can know how they are doing at a glance. And so I will get to show you how that works from a moving them a long standpoint. But each one of these is kind of like a class of people that starts at the same time. And you can have the date on the top so that you know exactly when they began. And you see the two is going to be the way out date. Now you see the highlight says on week five must schedule way out clipboard directly to right which is where you should have your last clipboard hanging which is this guy and then I will do the demo which is the way out schedule. So it looks identical to the way in schedule because it is the exact same thing except it says the word out next to it. And so there you just have different times than you have for the way in so that you can place people where you want them, where they can place themselves, where they can weigh out with you. And you might want to divide these up into 15 minute increments because that is kind of what I have seen on the back end because these are really last chance because you should be selling most of these people at the week three accountability meeting. So that is where 70% of your cells are actually going to happen for the conversion into EFT. It is not going to be on these way outs. It is still good for these people to schedule their way out so they can take pictures. You can get your before and afters, testimonials, all that kind of stuff. So it is important to make sure that they schedule this and that they show as an additional note people who are not on track with their weight. If you can get them to show up for the way out the likelihood that they sign up is incredibly high. So it is ironic but the people who do not hit but do show up for the way out are incredibly likely I think something to do with psychology of success like almost achieving something still feeling like there is unfinished business, it is an open loop, etc. If you can get those people to show you will crush the sales conversion percentages. So those are the sheets that I wanted to explain in the simple client accountability system.
